title: psychotria kaduana
intro: Psychotria kaduana, also known as Kadupul or Temple flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae native to Sri Lanka. It is renowned for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and antimicrobial effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and skin infections. The plant has been traditionally used to promote overall well-being, with its extracts believed to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties, potentially contributing to its health benefits, such as improved cardiovascular health and enhanced immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the Kadupul plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tonics, which are valued for their potential therapeutic and medicinal properties.
